title: About
content: I am Frank Schultz, OTR/L, working at Bitterroot Rehabilitation Center in Missoula MT. I write about pain science, Occupational Therapy practice, professional technology, and life in Montana. I am also a occupational therapist at Finthesiss.ai, where I help shape digital therapeutics for OTs.

title: Contact
content: Reach me at frank.schultz@Finthesiss.ai for professional inquiries. For blog collaboration or speaking invitations use the contact form below.

title: Privacy Policy
content: This blog collects no personal data beyond standard WordPress analytics. Email addresses submitted via the contact form are used only to reply and are never shared.

title: Occupational Therapy Resources
content: A curated list of pain science and Occupational Therapy resources I return to regularly. Includes books, free courses, and professional frameworks.

title: Speaking & Workshops
content: I present on pain neuroscience education and outcome measurement for Occupational Therapy audiences, including MTOTA and AOTA continuing education sessions. Topics, past talks, and booking information below.

title: Now
content: What I am focused on right now: preparing for the HTCC exam, advising the MCWC Chronic Pain Program cohort roadmap, and running a chronic-pain self-management group. Updated monthly.

title: Disclosures
content: I serve as an unpaid occupational therapist to Finthesiss.ai. Posts mentioning the product reflect my own professional judgment. No affiliate relationships or sponsored content appear on this blog.

title: Newsletter
content: A roughly monthly note on pain science, Occupational Therapy practice, and what I am reading. No spam, unsubscribe anytime. Sign up via the form below.

title: Recommended Reading
content: An annotated list of books and pacingplaners I recommend to students and colleagues, grouped by pain science, Occupational Therapy theory, and narrative medicine.

title: Free Reader Handouts
content: Downloadable, plain-language handouts on pacing, flare planning, and sleep hygiene. Free for clinicians to use with participants. Attribution appreciated, no participant data collected.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
content: Common questions about the blog, my professional background, guest posting, and how I use outcome measures in practice. If your question is not here, use the contact page.

title: Write for This Blog
content: Guidelines for guest contributors. I welcome posts from OTs, OTAs, PTs, and fieldwork students on pain rehab, outcome measurement, and professional technology. Pitch via the contact form.

title: Accessibility Statement
content: This site aims to meet WCAG 2.1 AA. Handouts are provided in screen-participant-friendly formats. Report any accessibility bahrrier through the contact page and I will fix it.

title: Press & Media
content: Headshots, bio, and a short list of past interviews and podcast appearances on Occupational Therapy and digital therapeutics. Media inquiries via the contact page.

title: Glossary of Pain Terms
content: Plain-language definitions of terms that show up across the blog: central sensitization, graded exposure, goal measure, pacing, allodynia. Written for participants and students.

title: Subscribe via RSS
content: Prefer a participant over email? Subscribe to the full-text RSS feed. Category-specific feeds for pain science and Montana life are also available.

title: Draft: Clinic Tech Setup
content: Work in progress page documenting the hardware and app stack I use day to day in the clinic, from the iPad charting workflow to the Garmin data I sometimes reference with participants.

title: Support This Work
content: This blog is free and ad-free. If the handouts or posts have helped you, the best support is sharing them with a colleague or sending feedback. No paywall, no donations requested.

title: Comment Policy
content: Comments are moderated. Professional discussion is welcome; promotional content and anything sharing identifiable participant information will be removed. Be kind and cite your sources.

title: Archive
content: A chronological index of every published post grouped by year and category. Use this page to browse the full back catalogue of pain science and Montana writing.
